title: Consumer Product Safety Publications - Canada.ca
content:
## Consumer Product Safety Publications
The resources below are available in HTML and PDF format. For more information, please contact the Consumer Product Safety Program using the general enquiries form , or by phone using our toll-free line 1-866-662-0666.
Safe sleep on the go
What to do if your baby falls asleep in a stroller, baby carrier or car seat.
Setting up a safe nursery
Baby room ideas, crib placement, securing furniture, toy safety; baby items your nursery doesn't need.
Dressing your baby for sleep
Safety tips for baby clothing designed for sleep, sleep sacks, and preventing overheating.
Cribs, cradles and bassinets
Tips for buying a crib, cradle or bassinet that's safe for your child.
Infant safe sleep tips for grandparents
Create a safe sleep environment for your grandbabies.
Safe sleep for your baby
Keep your baby safe for every sleep.
Safe sleep for your baby (poster)
Tips to help keep your baby safe for every sleep. Aimed at Indigenous parents, caregivers, and communities.
Safe sleep week – Tips for safe sleep #1
A poster showing 4 safe sleep tips for babies.
Safe sleep week – Tips for safe sleep #2
A poster showing 4 safe sleep tips for babies.
Joint statement on safe sleep: Reducing infant deaths in Canada
Evidence-based information to help health practitioners offer guidance to parents and caregivers on how to reduce the risk of infant sleep related death.
Series of 3 booklets that provide information on the safe use of consumer products for children.
Tips for safe baby wearing.
Activity Booklets and Game Board
Series of activity booklets and a game board related to safety messaging aimed at children 7 and under and 8 years old and up.
Safety information on the dangers associated with small, powerful magnets.
Button and lithium-ion battery toolkits
Factsheets, posters, postcards
A variety of resources with tips for reducing the risks associated with batteries.
Incident reporting toolkit
Posters, shareables, backgrounders, factsheets, infographics, videos
A variety of resources aimed at consumers and healthcare professionals on reporting incidents involving consumer products and cosmetics.
Facts for garage sale vendors
Tips for ensuring that the items you sell are safe and meet Canadian regulatory requirements.
Bringing consumer products into Canada
Information on products that are regulated and on products that are prohibited and can’t be brought into Canada.
Information for shoppers of second-hand products
Information on the risks and safety tips when buying second-hand products.
Tips for choosing the right helmet and how to wear helmets safely.
Winter sports and recreation safety
Tips for reducing the risk of injuries during winter sports and recreational activities.
Backyard trampoline and inflatable play structure safety
Tips for reducing the risk associated with backyard trampolines and inflatable play structures and steps for safer use.
